nameMeanings.name: Rokeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Coakley  .

variants.name: Coakley
meanings: Irish (Cork): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Caochlaoich, a patronymic from Caochlaoch, a personal name composed of the elements caoch ‘blind’ + laoch ‘warrior, hero’.
Irish: variant of Cloughley, possibly a shortened form of an unidentified Irish surname in Mac-.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Thomas Rokeley married Thomas Rokeley before 1268, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
